questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Coordination compounds are the result of the interaction of a central metal atom or ion with ligands.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Ligands are atoms, ions, or molecules that donate a pair of electrons to the central metal atom or ion.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and ligands donating a pair of electrons is the correct explanation of coordination compounds formation.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The coordination number of a metal ion in a complex is the number of donor atoms.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is the number of donor atoms surrounding the central metal atom or ion in a complex.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of coordination number.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The oxidation state of a metal ion in a complex is the same as the oxidation number of the metal ion in its elemental form.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Oxidation number of the metal ion in the complex is calculated by the charges of ligands and oxidation number of the metal ion in its elemental form.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion is false because the oxidation state of a metal ion in a complex can be different from the oxidation number of the metal ion in its elemental form.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The IUPAC name of a complex consists of the name of the ligands followed by the name of the metal.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a standard rule to name the ligands in alphabetical order followed by the name of the metal.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of IUPAC naming conventions.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The coordination geometry around a metal ion is determined by the number of electrons in the d subshell.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The coordination geometry is determined by the number of electrons in the d subshell and the nature of the ligands.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion is false because the coordination geometry is determined by the number of electrons in the d subshell and the nature of the ligands, but not just by the number of electrons in the d subshell.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The crystal field theory is a model used to explain the formation of complexes.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a model used to explain the splitting of d orbitals in the presence of ligands.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of crystal field theory.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The magnetic properties of a complex depend on the number of unpaired electrons in the central metal ion.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The magnetic properties of a complex depend on the number of unpaired electrons in the central metal ion and the nature of the ligands.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of magnetic properties.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The stability of a complex depends on the charge on the central metal ion.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The stability of a complex depends on the charge on the central metal ion and the nature of the ligands.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of stability of complexes.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The complexes with Werner's coordination number 6 have octahedral geometry.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The complexes with Werner's coordination number 6 have either octahedral or tetrahedral geometry.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion is false because the complexes with Werner's coordination number 6 can have either octahedral or tetrahedral geometry, not just octahedral geometry.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The ligands that can donate a pair of electrons are called sigma-donor ligands.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The sigma-donor ligands are the ligands that can donate a pair of electrons to the central metal atom or ion.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of sigma-donor ligands.
